messages.content:
[Sat 2026-07-04 21:59 PDT] [Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]

You are a BUILDER on the SwarmLab autonomous team. Workspace: /Users/beauxwalton/.openclaw/agents/scout/workspace/swarmlab

Beaux's PRIME RULE: only agents write code. This is a lab; the point is learning and weird emergent behavior.

STEP 1 — Orient (read these, in order): TEAM.md (the constitution), JOURNAL.md (what's been learned so far), then `git log --oneline -15`, and the existing built experiments experiments/02-adversarial-pair and experiments/04-consensus-under-lies as reference for the expected quality bar and how they use core/ (spawn, bus, score, trace, replay).

STEP 2 — Build experiment 06: `experiments/06-self-modifying-swarm/`. The spec is at specs/06-self-modifying-swarm.md — READ IT. Agents rewrite each other's system prompts mid-run based on a performance signal. Watch for runaway dynamics / degenerate attractors — this is the concrete argument for why Lattice gate policy exists. Keep it CONTAINED per the spec's safety rails (prompt-size cap, hard round limit, kill-switch on collapse, no modification outside the sandbox). Trace every mutation through core/ so it's fully replayable.

Follow the Definition of Done in TEAM.md exactly:
- Typechecks clean (npm run typecheck from repo root or the experiment dir), no `any` in public signatures.
- Runs, produces valid JSONL trace in runs/, replay() reads it back.
- Write a real README.md documenting what you actually observed (does it stabilize/oscillate/collapse? what attractors?). NEVER fake a green result — a real red trace beats a fake green one.
- Append a one-paragraph learning note to JOURNAL.md.
- COMMIT AND PUSH to origin/main after the green build (git add, commit with clear message, git push). If push fails: git pull --rebase origin main then push. Committing+pushing is mandatory — session recycling can abort long runs…
